active_blocker: A733 SDMMC0 reaches card enumeration and partition table, but normal block I/O stalls on large CMD18. The latest IDMA runs stop at COMMAND_DONE|RX_DATA_REQUEST with IDST=0x4000, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, and BBCR=0.

operator_model.restore_after_test: Power-cycle Cubie3 and verify vendor kernel 5.15.147-21-a733 before ending the session.

session_exit_gates.hypothesis_queue_update_committed: Update and commit this file. Sync it to /srv/projects/homelab/task-packets/kernel/a733-hypothesis-queue.json on Strix.
session_exit_gates.remote_push_checked: Push repos with configured remotes and record any repo that has no remote.

guardrails: Do not add vendor U-Boot compatibility strings, aliases, paths, or policy nodes to upstream DTS.
Do not expand into Ethernet, VPU, display, USB-C, PCIe, or wireless.
Do not submit local A733 CCU or pinctrl scaffolding while Junhui Liu's CCU/PRCM RFC and Andre Przywara's pinctrl RFC are in flight.
Keep clk_ignore_unused and PIO bypasses as lab diagnostics only.

latest_completed_test.title: Use vendor SDMMC0 MSI Lite1 clock gate instead of local MSI Lite0 mapping
latest_completed_test.result: Vendor A733 DTS wires SDMMC0's mmc_msi_lite clock to CLK_MSI_LITE1, which maps to the local RFC gate CLK_BUS_MSI_LITE1 at 0x59c bit 0. Changing only the local SDMMC0 mmc_msi_lite consumer from CLK_MBUS_MSI_LITE0 to CLK_BUS_MSI_LITE1 does not fix the first large CMD18. The boot still stops with RINTR=0x24, IDST=0x4000, CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, CBCR=0x400, and BBCR=0. This falsifies the vendor-MSI-Lite1-clock-mapping hypothesis for the current IDMA stall.

hypotheses.title: Fixed vendor-vs-mainline data-phase register comparison before another boot variant
hypotheses.question: Which visible SDMMC0 register differs at the same data-phase moment where vendor advances CHDA/CBDA and mainline remains in descriptor-read state?
hypotheses.test_cost: low; source/log analysis first, no kernel build required
procedure: Read the vendor SDMMC0 wide register sample and the latest mainline UART log.
Fill task-packets/kernel/a733-sdmmc-register-dump-template.json for the phases vendor-working-read, mainline-dma-entry, mainline-idma-post-cmdr, and mainline-stall-poll.

Compare GCTRL, CLKCR, WIDTH, CMDR, CARG, IMASK, RINTR, STAS, NTSR, FTRGL, THLD, DMAC, DLBA, IDST, IDIE, CHDA, CBDA, BLKSZ, BCNTR, CBCR, BBCR, FUNS, DBGC, CSDC, DRV_DL, SAMP_DL, DS_DL, SKEW_CTRL, and SMCV.
If a single non-cosmetic delta remains, create the next minimal patch from that delta only. If no visible delta remains, move to H002.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A register delta is identified that plausibly controls data/DMA progress and is absent from prior falsified tests.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No visible register delta remains; next work must inspect hidden wrapper/source behavior instead of cycling another visible-register boot variant.

result.summary: Most visible setup registers match: CMDR, FTRGL, THLD, DMAC, IDIE, FUNS, DRV_DL, NTSR, SMCV. Vendor advances CHDA/CBDA and data counters, while mainline remains at CHDA=DLBA and CBDA=0. The only setup-looking visible delta is SAMP_DL=0x00002000 on vendor versus 0 on mainline, but the raw SAMP_DL write was already tested and did not stick. Do not create another visible-register boot variant without source evidence.

hypotheses.title: Find hidden v5p3x wrapper, timing-phase, or MBUS/storage behavior in vendor source
hypotheses.question: Does the vendor A733 v5p3x path program a non-SDMMC register, wrapper bit, or timing phase path required before IDMAC can fetch descriptors?
hypotheses.test_cost: low to medium; source search before build
partial_results: Orange Pi A733 v5p3x source has a direct REG_SAMP_DL programming block, but it is commented out.
The active vendor phase path writes REG_SD_NTSR and saves/restores REG_SAMP_DL; the v5p3x init path does not assign the sunxi_mmc_set_samp_dl hook.
The preserved-SAMP_DL diagnostic proves SAMP_DL=0x00002000 survives into the failing mainline CMD18, but IDMAC still sticks in descriptor-read state.
Explicit SDMMC0 consumers for mmc_store, mmc_mbus, and mmc_msi_lite were already tested and did not fix IDMA.
Vendor A733 DTS uses CLK_MSI_LITE1 for SDMMC0; the local diagnostic changed only the SDMMC0 mmc_msi_lite consumer to CLK_BUS_MSI_LITE1 and still hit the same CMD18 IDMA descriptor-read stall.
procedure: Continue the vendor/source search outside the already-falsified direct SAMP_DL, sample-calibration, and explicit mmc_store/mmc_mbus/mmc_msi_lite paths.
Search for SDMMC0, v5p3x, IDMAC, MBUS, MSI-lite, storage fabric, wrapper register writes outside the SDMMC register window, cache/MBUS attributes, and any A733-specific IDMAC enable sequence.
Record file, function, register address, value, and call path.
Only if a concrete write is found, build a minimal diagnostic that adds that one write with printk evidence.
Boot Cubie3 once, capture UART, restore vendor kernel, and update this queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: A specific hidden register/write is found and tested.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: No source-backed hidden write exists in available vendor code.

hypotheses.title: Lower-level CMD17 proof outside mmcblk queue setup
hypotheses.question: Can a single-block read complete cleanly when issued as a targeted diagnostic instead of forcing block-layer queue geometry?
hypotheses.test_cost: medium; small kernel patch and one boot
procedure: Do not change global mmcblk queue limits.
Add a one-shot diagnostic after card init that issues or emulates one controlled 512-byte read and logs RINTR, STAS, FIFO, and data bytes.
Restore Cubie3 and update the queue.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: Controlled CMD17 completes and returns data, separating single-block protocol from CMD18/multi-block behavior.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: CMD17 also stops at command-done/RX-request with no data-over.

hypotheses.title: Retest descriptor geometry, descriptor size, address shifting, DIC, chain bits, CEATA, DMAC reset, fabric-clock order, or vendor delay
hypotheses.question: Previously tested visible descriptor/control variants
hypotheses.test_cost: wasteful unless new source evidence changes the expected value
procedure: Do not rerun these variants as exploratory guesses.
Only reopen a subcase if H001/H002 identifies a concrete mismatch or missing vendor behavior.
fail_history: v5p3x 4 KiB descriptor segmentation still stalls.
DIC and chain-bit variants still stall.
CEATA omission and DMAC reset variants still stall.
Vendor-like DRV_DL does not fix CMD18.
Explicit descriptor dma_sync_single_for_device does not fix CMD18.
Preserving vendor/bootloader SAMP_DL=0x00002000 and skipping mainline sample calibration does not fix CMD18.
Using the vendor SDMMC0 MSI Lite1 clock gate via CLK_BUS_MSI_LITE1 does not fix CMD18.
